Amendments1984—Par. (2). amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “Any still, boiler, or other vessel to be used for the purpose of distilling which is removed without notice having been given as required by section 5105(a) or which is set up without permit first having been obtained as required by such section; and”.Pub. L. 98–3691979—Par. (5). substituted “distilled spirits plant” for “distillery, bonded warehouse, or rectifying or bottling establishment” in three places.Pub. L. 96–39

(2) read as follows: “Any still, boiler, or other vessel to be used for the purpose of distilling which is removed without notice having been given as required by section 5105(a) or which is set up without permit first having been obtained as required by such section; and”.Pub. Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective on first day of first calendar month which begins more than 90 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 98–369section 456(a) of Pub. L. 98–369section 5101 of this titleJuly 18, 1984

children.heading: Unregistered still or distilling apparatus
children.label: 1 Unregistered still or distilling apparatus
children.text: Every still or distilling apparatus not registered as required by section 5179, together with all personal property in the possession or custody or under the control of the person required by section 5179 to register the still or distilling apparatus, and found in the building or in any yard or inclosure connected with the building in which such still or distilling apparatus is set up; and

children.heading: Distilling apparatus removed without notice or set up without notice
children.label: 2 Distilling apparatus removed without notice or set up without notice
A
children.text: which is removed without notice having been given when required by section 5101(a)(1), or

children.label: B
children.text: which is set up without notice having been given when required by section 5101(a)(2); and

children.heading: Distilling without giving bond or with intent to defraud
children.label: 3 Distilling without giving bond or with intent to defraud
A
children.text: all distilled spirits or wines, and all stills or other apparatus fit or intended to be used for the distillation or rectification of spirits, or for the compounding of liquors, owned by such person, wherever found; and

children.label: B
children.text: all distilled spirits, wines, raw materials for the production of distilled spirits, and personal property found in the distillery or in any building, room, yard, or inclosure connected therewith and used with or constituting a part of the premises; and

children.label: C
children.text: all the right, title, and interest of such person in the lot or tract of land on which the distillery is situated; and

children.label: D
children.text: all the right, title, and interest in the lot or tract of land on which the distillery is located of every person who knowingly has suffered or permitted the business of a distiller to be there carried on, or has connived at the same; and

children.label: E
children.text: all personal property owned by or in possession of any person who has permitted or suffered any building, yard, or inclosure, or any part thereof, to be used for purposes of ingress or egress to or from the distillery, which shall be found in any such building, yard, or inclosure; and

children.label: F
children.text: all the right, title, and interest of every person in any premises used for ingress or egress to or from the distillery who knowingly has suffered or permitted such premises to be used for such ingress or egress; and

children.heading: Unlawful production and removals from vinegar plants
children.label: 4 Unlawful production and removals from vinegar plants
A
children.text: all distilled spirits in excess of 15 percent of alcohol by volume produced on the premises of a vinegar plant; and

children.label: B
children.text: all vinegar or other fluid or other material containing a greater proportion than 2 percent of proof spirits removed from any vinegar plant; and

children.heading: False or omitted entries in records, returns, and reports
children.label: 5 False or omitted entries in records, returns, and reports
A
children.text: fail to keep any such document or to make required entries therein; or

children.label: B
children.text: make any false entry in such document; or

children.label: C
children.text: cancel, alter, or obliterate any part of such document, or any entry therein, or destroy any part of such document, or entry therein; or

children.label: D
children.text: hinder or obstruct any internal revenue officer from inspecting any such document or taking any abstracts therefrom; or

children.label: E
children.text: fail or refuse to preserve or produce any such document, as required by this chapter or regulations issued pursuant thereto; or

children.label: F
children.text: permit any of the acts described in the preceding subparagraphs to be performed;

children.heading: Unlawful removal of distilled spirits
children.label: 6 Unlawful removal of distilled spirits
children.text: All distilled spirits on which the tax has not been paid or determined which have been removed, other than as authorized by law, from the place of manufacture, storage, or instrument of transportation; and

children.heading: Creation of fictitious proof
children.label: 7 Creation of fictitious proof
children.text: All distilled spirits on which the tax has not been paid or determined as provided by law to which any ingredient or substance has been added for the purpose of creating fictitious proof.
